subject: Chemistry
title: Lithium: From Rocks to Batteries
description: One-minute skill: Lithium: From Rocks to Batteries.

hook.text: Lithium is the lightest metal on Earth. It floats on water—right before it reacts and catches fire.

buildup.text: Discovered in 1817 in a mineral from a Swedish mine, its name comes from 'lithos'—Greek for stone.

discovery.text: Lithium's tiny size lets its ions move easily through materials, making it perfect for rechargeable batteries.

twist.text: It also treats bipolar disorder. Nobody fully understands why it stabilizes mood, but it works.

climax.text: Lithium powers phones, laptops, and electric cars. Demand has made it 'white gold.'

punchline.text: A metal from stone that powers the modern world.

quiz.question: What makes lithium ideal for rechargeable batteries?
options: Its tiny ions move easily through materials
